subagent-prompt.task:
Audit a sandcastle session transcript. Two modes — pick the right one based on the focus-area input:
- **PROPOSAL mode** (use when focus-area is a specific change, e.g. "add /simplify to the loop", "make X work for other projects"): Output a focused proposal addressing exactly that ask. Don't pad with unrelated issues or discipline lessons. - **FULL AUDIT mode** (use when focus-area is "none — survey all issues"): Identify every sandcastle issue (what broke / how caused / how fixed / prevention) and every assistant diagnostic mistake in the transcript.
If unsure, prefer PROPOSAL — the user can always re-run with no argument for a full audit.

inputs.is-sandcastle-template-repo: {{yes or no — yes only if this is the sandcastle-loop template itself, not a downstream project using it}}

rules.rule: Read the export file fully before making any claim. Do not rely on summarized impressions.
Reference PRDs, plans, ADRs, issues, commits, audit files by path or URL only. Never copy their content into the audit.

subagent-prompt.verification-discipline:
Every claim in the audit must carry one of four tags.
Someone said it in the transcript. No evidence checked. Transcript itself contains the evidence — commit hash + log output, file content quoted, error message reproduced. Include the quote inline. `git log` / `git show` on the local repo confirms the claim. Must include the command, the absolute path it ran in, and `git rev-parse HEAD` at that path. Grep or read of the file confirms current state. Must use an absolute path. Banned: relative paths in verification commands — they caused a past audit to falsely declare "fix not in code" because cwd had shifted.
Before any code-derived finding, run and record in the audit: - `pwd` - `git rev-parse HEAD` - absolute path of the file you intend to check
Distinguish "fix landed in git history" (VERIFIED-IN-GIT) from "fix is on disk right now" (VERIFIED-IN-CODE). These are different claims. A local checkout may be behind upstream (VPS without recent /sandcastle-update). VERIFIED-IN-GIT pass + VERIFIED-IN-CODE fail = "you need to update," not "fix doesn't exist."
subagent-prompt.required-output-structure:
Always include the Header and References sections. Pick the rest based on mode.
Host, working directory, is-sandcastle-template-repo, focus-area, mode (PROPOSAL or FULL AUDIT), export-file path, audit-file path, timestamp, transcript-mode (TRANSCRIPT or RECONSTRUCTED — read from the export's first line, which is `MODE: TRANSCRIPT` or `MODE: RECONSTRUCTED`).
Address the focus-area directly. Three short sub-sections: - **The change** — what to add/modify in the sandcastle template (file paths + brief description of edit). - **Why** — the one-paragraph justification (cite the transcript turn that motivated it). - **Rollout** — which files need the change (active prompt + variants? main.mts? a skill?), what tests/verification apply, and what the user should run to propagate (usually `/sandcastle-update` in other projects after the template ships). Keep it tight — a focused proposal beats a sprawling audit. Skip Sandcastle Issues and Assistant Mistakes unless they directly bear on the proposal.
For each issue (one sub-section per issue): - What broke (symptom the user saw) - How sandcastle caused it (which loop behaviour, file, or assumption) - How it was fixed in-session (commands run, files changed) - Prevention (what should change in the template to stop recurrence) Each fact tagged per verification-discipline.
For each diagnostic mistake (one sub-section per): - What was asserted - What disproved it (with evidence quote) - Discipline-fix (the rule that would have caught it, e.g. "run `git diff HEAD` before concluding")
Include a mistake ONLY if its discipline-fix would be encoded in the sandcastle template, the sandcastle skills, or sandcastle prompts. Exclude mistakes whose fix lives in the user's CLAUDE.md, general session discipline, or unrelated tooling — those belong in a different audit. Examples to EXCLUDE: "should have run /clear between unrelated tasks", "didn't check existing PRD before creating new one", "surface-level UI review", "used technical jargon despite user preferences". Examples to INCLUDE: "implementer claimed e2e ran when it didn't" (template can add stricter cert check), "/sandcastle-update said 'no changes' with no SHA evidence" (skill can require SHA in output), "loop crashed on missing image" (preflight can catch). If after filtering there are zero in-scope mistakes, write "(none — assistant mistakes this session are outside sandcastle's scope)" and move on. Do NOT pad with general-session items.
What to investigate or implement next. Which skills to invoke (e.g. `diagnose`, `tdd`, `grill-with-docs`, `sandcastle-update`). Which sandcastle-repo files to read first. In PROPOSAL mode this often collapses to "implement the proposal above in the template repo and push."
Paths and URLs only. PRDs, plans, ADRs, issues, commits, audit files. Never copy content.
subagent-prompt.return-value: Write the full audit to the audit-output-file. Return to the main agent a 2–4 sentence plain-English summary of the top issue and recommended fix.
